title: Verify chain guards .bee/bin/** against the onboarding.json managed-hash ledger (P1)
action: USER-REPORTED, and it shipped to main: a clean checkout of main reported `drift: true` for .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs at version 1.9.0 while the files were byte-identical to their source — only the TRACKED .bee/onboarding.json still held the pre-fix hash, because the release ran onboarding and the tag was then re-pointed to a later fix commit that changed .bee/bin/** without re-onboarding (fixed by hand in 6412017). Root gap: this repo has THREE copy relationships and only two are guarded — `skills/bee-hive/templates/** <-> .bee/bin/**` has scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs, and the plugin trees have release_manifest --check, but `.bee/bin/** <-> .bee/onboarding.json managed hashes` has NOTHING, so a stale ledger ships silently and then lies to every new session about its install. Build the missing guard, same idiom as release_manifest --check: a check that recomputes the managed hashes for every file the ledger claims and FAILS when any recorded hash is stale, a claimed file is missing, or an unrecorded .mjs sits in the fully-managed lib dir. Reuse the SAME hasher the recorder uses (onboard_bee.mjs buildManagedVersions / the hashFile used by computeRuntimeDrift in bee.mjs) so recorder and checker can never disagree — do not write a second hashing implementation. Wire it into the verify chain (scripts/run_verify.mjs discovery, following how release_manifest --check is wired) so a release can never tag a commit whose ledger was not refreshed. The refusal must name the stale files and the one-line fix (re-run self-onboard). RED-FIRST: write the check's own test first (fixture repo: a ledger recording a hash that no longer matches -> FAIL; missing claimed file -> FAIL; unrecorded extra lib .mjs -> FAIL; a freshly-onboarded fixture -> PASS), run red, record `cells verify --id lpsp-1 --passed false`, then implement. Prove additionally against THIS repo: the check passes now (ledger was just refreshed) and fails if a byte of .bee/bin/lib/*.mjs is changed without re-onboarding. TARGETED verify only.

truths: a stale recorded hash, a missing claimed file, and an unrecorded extra lib .mjs each FAIL the check
the check uses the same hasher as the recorder — no second implementation
it runs inside the verify chain, so a release cannot tag a stale ledger
the failure message names the offending files and says to re-run self-onboard
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never auto-refresh the ledger from the check — it reports, the human/CLI fixes (a self-healing check hides the ordering bug it exists to catch)
Do not write a second hashing implementation

trace.outcome: The third copy relationship is now guarded: the verify chain fails when .bee/onboarding.json's managed hashes go stale, so a release cannot tag a commit whose ledger was not refreshed
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":["scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs and release_manifest --check — the two guards that already existed, whose idiom this follows"],"tests_added":["scripts/test_ledger_parity.mjs on fixture repos: stale recorded hash FAILS and names the file, claimed file missing FAILS, unrecorded extra lib .mjs FAILS, missing ledger FAILS cleanly without throwing, freshly-onboarded PASSES, and one case asserting THIS repo's real ledger passes"],"red_failure_evidence":"Before the checker existed the suite could not even load: ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for scripts/ledger_parity.mjs, exit 1 (trace --passed false). The condition it now catches shipped for real on main in commit 1aa74de and was reported by the user as drift:true on a clean checkout at 1.9.0 while every file was byte-identical to source.","verification_run":"Worker + orchestrator both reproduced the live proof on this repo: check green; append one byte to .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs without re-onboarding -> MISMATCH stale, exit 1; git checkout restores it -> green again, repo left clean. Wired into run_verify EXTRA_SUITES beside release_manifest --check; test_verify_manifest still green (60 suites discovered, 24 mandatory present). Uses fsutil's hashFile — the same function the recorder uses, no second hasher. Report-only: it never refreshes the ledger, because self-healing would hide the ordering bug it exists to catch."}
trace.verify_output:
PASS freshly-onboarded tree PASSES
PASS stale recorded hash FAILS and names the file
PASS claimed file missing FAILS and names the file
PASS unrecorded extra .mjs in the fully-managed lib dir FAILS
PASS missing onboarding.json ledger FAILS with a clear error, never throws
PASS THIS repo's real ledger currently passes (proves the check runs against reality, not just fixtures)

PASS scripts/test_ledger_parity.mjs
EXIT_CODE:0

Additional proof against THIS repo (not part of the automated suite): node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check passed clean; appending a byte to .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs made it FAIL (MISMATCH stale .bee/bin/lib/lock.mjs, exit 1); git checkout restored the byte (md5 5c7e689e41f05e6eda506d62fd1f2ba0 before and after) and the check passed again, exit 0. Repo left unmodified by that probe.
